eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls from AtlasIntel, Datafolha, and Quaest in late April 2026 show incumbent President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva leading first-round scenarios at 37-47%, with Senator Flávio Bolsonaro consolidating opposition support in clear second place at 32-40%, well ahead of trailing candidates like Ronaldo Caiado, Romeu Zema, and Renan Santos under 6%. Trader consensus pricing Flávio Bolsonaro at 68% implied probability for second reflects his endorsement by ineligible former President Jair Bolsonaro, the April 4 resignation deadline withdrawals by rivals including Tarcísio de Freitas and Fernando Haddad, and a fragmented field likely forcing an October 25 runoff. Lula's 19.5% for second underscores low odds of him slipping amid stable polling trends.

markets.question: Will US crude oil reserves fall to 325M by June 5?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the U.S. Energy Information Administration publishes a weekly figure for U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve less than or equal to the specified value for any week ending on or before June 5, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve as soon as the listed value is reached, or once data has been released for the final week ending on or before June 5, 2026, and the listed value has not been reached.

If data has not been released for the final week ending on or before June 5 2026, by June 12, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on the data available at that time.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the U.S. Energy Information Administration, specifically the weekly data published for the U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve at https://www.eia.gov/dnav/pet/hist/LeafHandler.ashx?n=PET&s=WCSSTUS1&f=W.

Note: this market’s resolution source publishes weekly values of U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve in thousands of barrels. Thus, this will be the level of specificity used to resolve this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: US crude oil inventories plunged 6.2 million barrels to 459.5 million barrels in the week ending April 24—the largest draw in months—driven by surging exports and steady refinery inputs averaging 16.1 million barrels per day, amid Brent prices spiking to $103 per barrel from Middle East military actions tightening global supply. Earlier April reports showed builds exceeding forecasts, reflecting softer demand, but seasonal summer driving season could accelerate draws through June. Traders track weekly EIA Petroleum Status Reports (next: May 6 for week ending May 2), culminating in the June 4 release for week ending June 3, which determines resolution by June 5, alongside API previews Tuesdays and potential OPEC+ output decisions.

markets.question: Will Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) hit (HIGH) $7.00 Week of May 4 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during the week of May 4 2026, any 1-minute candle for Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.OPEN%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.OPEN%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) hit Week of May 4 2026?
events.description: What will Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) hit Week of May 4 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) shares closed at $5.38 on April 30, 2026, trading above the analyst consensus price target of approximately $4.80 amid anticipation for its Q1 2026 earnings release on May 7—directly within the market week. The iBuying platform's performance hinges on U.S. housing dynamics, with 30-year mortgage rates easing to 6.23% and forecasts for 8-10% inventory growth supporting potential home sales rebound. Q4 2025 results beat expectations with EPS of -$0.07 versus consensus -$0.10, boosting revenue trends, though persistent losses and competitive pressures cap upside. Traders eye earnings for contribution profit guidance and housing volume metrics, with volatility likely around the post-market report.

markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in Hong Kong be 17°C on April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the lowest temperature recorded by the Hong Kong Observatory in degrees Celsius on 30 Apr '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the Hong Kong Observatory, specifically the "Absolute Daily Min (deg. C)" the specified date once information is finalized in the relevant "Daily Extract", available here: https://www.weather.gov.hk/en/cis/climat.htm

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures in Celsius to one decimal place (eg, 9.1°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: A month into the 2026 MLB season, Cleveland Guardians lefty Parker Messick tops trader consensus at 24.8% implied probability for AL Cy Young thanks to his 3-0 record, 1.73 ERA, 0.88 WHIP, and 38 strikeouts over 36.1 innings, fueling breakout buzz among Guardians rotation hopefuls. Tarik Skubal follows closely at 21.5% as the defending two-time winner with a 3-2 mark, 2.70 ERA, and league-leading 45 punchouts in seven starts for Detroit, bolstered by MLB.com's recent poll naming him the early favorite. New York Yankees rookie Cam Schlittler at 18.0% boasts the AL's best 1.53 ERA and 1.7 WAR through 41.2 frames with 41 Ks, highlighted by an eight-strikeout gem on April 28. The tight clustering reflects small-sample volatility, elite peripherals across the trio, Seattle's Bryce Miller sidelined on the injured list until late May, and strong bids from Jose Soriano's league-high WAR and Dylan Cease's consistency, with the long season ahead amplifying uncertainty in innings, wins, and sustained dominance.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Houston be between 78-79°F on May 3?
markets.description:

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the William P. Hobby Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/tx/houston/KHOU.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

events.title: Highest temperature in Houston on May 3?
events.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the William P. Hobby Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 3 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the William P. Hobby Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/tx/houston/KHOU.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following a cold front's passage on May 1-2 that delivered unseasonably cool, dry air to southeast Texas, National Weather Service forecasts now call for sunny skies over Houston on May 3 with light north winds 5-10 mph and highs near 80°F at William P. Hobby Airport, fueling trader consensus at 67% combined implied probability for the 78-81°F range. The tight race between 78-79°F (35%) and 80-81°F (32%) stems from ensemble model spread—GFS and ECMWF guidance clustering around 79-81°F—hinging on boundary layer heating rates under full insolation versus any persistent cool air advection or afternoon cumulus development. New 00Z model runs overnight could refine peak temperature differentials, with climatological May 3 norms at 83°F providing upside context amid building high pressure.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket overwhelmingly favors a high temperature of 66°F or higher in Austin on May 4 (99.6% implied probability), driven by National Weather Service forecast guidance showing abundant sunshine and highs in the middle to upper 70s amid a post-cold snap warming trend. After May 1's unusually cool high—one of Austin's coldest for the month—upper-level ridging is building over Texas, suppressing cool air intrusions and promoting adiabatic warming under clear skies and light southerly winds. Ensemble models like GFS and ECMWF exhibit strong agreement on this outcome, with climatological May averages near 83°F providing robust baseline support. Realistic challenges include an unexpected mid-level trough diving southward, though current steering patterns and jet stream positioning make this improbable; watch Sunday's 12Z model updates for confirmation.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the IL-09 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the IL-09 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Illinois' 9th Congressional District, a longtime Democratic stronghold with a partisan voting index exceeding D+20, favors Democratic nominee Daniel Biss following his victory in the crowded March 17 primary over 15 challengers, solidifying party support in the wake of Rep. Jan Schakowsky's retirement. Biss enters the November 3 general election with strong fundraising—over $250,000 banked in early April—and endorsements, contrasting weak Republican nominee John Elleson, a pastor with limited resources in a district last won by a GOP candidate decades ago. Trader consensus prices a Democratic victory at 92.5%, reflecting historical margins and low GOP turnout. Upsets remain possible via a major Biss scandal, national Republican midterm wave, or voting irregularities, though barriers are high.

markets.question: Will Airbnb, Inc. (ABNB) hit (LOW) $116 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Airbnb, Inc. (ABNB) has a final "Low" price equal to or below the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Airbnb, Inc. (ABNB) "Low" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.ABNB%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.ABNB%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily low price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Airbnb, Inc. (ABNB) hit in May 2026?
events.description: What will Airbnb, Inc. (ABNB) hit in May 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Airbnb (ABNB) shares trade around $142, up 12% over the past month and near the 52-week high of $147, fueled by optimism ahead of the Q1 2026 earnings release on May 7. Q4 2025 results showed revenue growth accelerating to 12% year-over-year at $2.78 billion, beating estimates despite an EPS miss at $0.56, with full-year revenue reaching $12.2 billion on robust gross bookings and nights booked expansion. Analyst consensus rates the stock overweight with an average 12-month price target of $150, reflecting expectations for continued travel demand recovery and AI-driven innovations. Q1 guidance of $2.59–$2.63 billion revenue (14–16% growth) sets the bar; outperformance or upbeat Q2 outlook could propel shares higher by May's end, while softening occupancy or macro headwinds pose risks.

events.title: Albacete Balompié vs. Cultural y Deportiva Leonesa - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the La Liga 2 game, scheduled for May 9 at 10:15 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Albacete Balompié hosts relegation-battling Cultural y Deportiva Leonesa in LaLiga 2 on May 9 at Estadio Carlos Belmonte, with trader consensus likely favoring the mid-table hosts due to home advantage and Leonesa's dismal form. Leonesa sit 22nd after recent defeats, including 2-1 at Mirandés on April 26, 1-2 home loss to Córdoba on April 18, and 1-0 at Granada on April 12, leaving them desperate for points in a must-win survival push amid their 8-8-20 record. Albacete, 13th in the table, lost 0-3 at Eibar on April 24 but won 4-1 versus Granada prior, showing attacking threat at home. Head-to-head history tilts toward Leonesa with two prior wins and three draws, though low-scoring (0.8 goals average); no major injuries reported, but long-term absences like Albacete's Antonio Puertas persist. Late-season stakes heighten upset potential for the visitors.

Both sides limp into the clash on poor form, Sevilla with one win in five (L-L-W-L-L, no clean sheet in eight) and Espanyol drawing blanks recently (L-D-L-L-D), yet Sevilla's superior head-to-head record (20 wins to five) and Espanyol's season-ending absence of striker Javi Puado from a cruciate ligament tear bolster the hosts' implied 45% probability. Sevilla miss centre-back Marcão (scaphoid fracture) and others like Djibril Sow, keeping draw odds competitive at 27% in this low-scoring matchup.

markets.question: Will Anže Logar be the next Prime Minister of Slovenia?

eventMetadata.context_description: Following Robert Golob's April 20 announcement that his Freedom Movement could not form a coalition government after narrowly winning the March 22 parliamentary elections, Slovenia remains in post-election deadlock with no majority in the 90-seat National Assembly. President Nataša Pirc Musar declined to nominate a prime minister candidate on April 25 following consultations, opening the path for runner-up Janez Janša of the Slovenian Democratic Party (SDS) to lead coalition talks with center-right partners like New Slovenia (NSi, led by Jernej Vrtovec). Traders price Janša at 88.6% implied probability, reflecting his prior experience as three-time premier and SDS's strong second-place showing, while low odds on Golob and others signal failed left-center negotiations; a National Assembly confidence vote on the next mandate holder looms, with snap election risk minimal per market consensus.

markets.question: Will Claude go down 12+ times in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of days in the month of May during which Claude (claude.ai) experiences any downtime.

Claude will be considered to have experienced downtime on a given day if the Claude Status historical uptime page for the claude.ai component (https://status.claude.com/uptime/) displays that day’s status box as any color other than green once the day’s status box is finalized. Daily status boxes for Claude components other than claude.ai will not impact this market’s resolution.

A day will be considered finalized once the following day’s status box has a display color other than the grey color used for days which have not yet begun.

This market will resolve once the final day in May 2026 is finalized. If the final day in May 2026 has not been finalized by June 7, 2026 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on the days in May which have been finalized up to that time.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the Claude Status historical uptime page for the claude.ai component (https://status.claude.com/uptime/).

These incidents, affecting Claude.ai, API access, and Claude Code with 500 server errors and login failures, highlight ongoing infrastructure scaling challenges amid surging demand for the large language model. Status.anthropic.com reports no May issues yet as of May 2, but repeated April failures signal persistent reliability risks, positioning 9-11 days (22%) as the next likely bin. Traders eye Anthropic's investigations and potential scaling updates for shifts before month-end resolution.

markets.question: Mobile Legends Bang Bang: Geek Fam ID vs EVOS (BO3) - MPL Indonesia Regular Season
markets.description:
This market refers to the Mobile Legends Bang Bang match between Geek Fam ID and EVOS in the MPL Indonesia Regular Season, initially scheduled for May 3 at 9:15AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Geek Fam ID" if Geek Fam ID win the match against EVOS.

This market will resolve to "EVOS" if EVOS win the match against Geek Fam ID.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the MPL Indonesia Season 17 regular season double round robin, EVOS (5-5, fifth place) holds a slight edge over Geek Fam ID (4-6, seventh) in this pivotal BO3 matchup for playoff positioning among the top six teams. EVOS dominated their Week 1 head-to-head 2-0 and boosted momentum with a 2-1 upset over Alter Ego yesterday, showcasing strong teamfight execution. Geek Fam ID, hampered by inconsistency including a reverse-sweep 1-2 loss to Alter Ego on Friday despite stealing game one, relies on import Baloyskie's initiation and recent resilience like their April 26 win over ONIC. Roster stability persists post early-season tweaks, amid Patch 2.1.61's annual map changes favoring adaptable drafts in this closely contested mid-table clash.

markets.question: Will Reform UK win control of the most London borough councils?
markets.description:
Local elections in London, UK are currently scheduled for May 7, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the party that controls the most London borough councils as a result of this election.

Control is defined as having more than half of the voting councillors of a London borough council. Any executive positions, like Mayor, will not count.

A candidate will be considered an official candidate of a party if they are officially nominated by that party and are registered for the relevant election in affiliation with that party. Independent candidates will not count for any party.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of councils controlled by the specified party, not any coalition of which it may be a part. 

In the case of a tie between two or more parties for the greatest number of councils controlled, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose name comes first in alphabetical order, as listed in this market group.

Resolution of this market will be based on the results of the relevant London borough council elections, once those results are official. This market may resolve to the listed party as soon as it becomes mathematically impossible for any other party to equal or surpass its number of councils controlled in these elections. If the results of any of the relevant 2026 London local elections aren’t known by April 30, 2027 11:59 PM ET, the winning party will be determined based on the available results up to that point. If none of the results of the relevant 2026 London local elections are known by that time, this market will resolve to “Other”.

This market will resolve based on the results of the elections as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the respective council government.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent YouGov MRP polling released April 22 projects Labour leading vote shares in 15 of 32 London boroughs—down six from 2022 but far ahead of rivals—ahead of the May 7 local elections, anchoring trader consensus at 82.5% implied probability for the party securing control of the most councils despite national mid-term fatigue and Gaza-related discontent eroding its inner-city base. Greens, surging to 22% London-wide (up 10 points), are favored at 16% after polls forecast them topping Hackney, Lambeth, Lewisham, and Waltham Forest amid young voter shifts from Labour. Reform UK gains in outer boroughs like Barking and Dagenham erode Conservatives, while Liberal Democrats hold southwest strengths; high fragmentation risks no-overall-control outcomes, though Labour's incumbency and first-past-the-post dynamics favor retained pluralities.

markets.question: Abstract FDV above $200M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Abstract's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Abstract doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Karachi be 31°C on May 3?

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Masroor Airbase Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/pk/karachi/OPKC.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will ByteDance have the #3 AI model at the end of May 2026 (Style Control On)?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the third-highest arena rank (Style Control On) based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies third place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

Jerry Nadler's retirement after 32 years representing NY-12, the June 23 Democratic primary remains closely contested among state Assemblymembers Micah Lasher and Alex Bores, with Jack Schlossberg third on Kennedy family name recognition and social media buzz. Lasher's trader-implied lead at 38.5% stems from Nadler's February endorsement as his former aide, bolstering establishment support in this Manhattan stronghold, while Bores trails narrowly at 34% on progressive appeals and internal polling strength. Recent April forums at 92NY and legal groups, Schlossberg's first ad on April 29, and the Working Families Party's rejection of Schlossberg underscore field fragmentation; late endorsements from unions or elected officials, external polls, or turnout among key voting blocs like Upper West Side progressives could tip the balance in this low-turnout primary.

markets.question: Will Yoo Dong-soo win the 2026 Incheon mayoral election?

eventMetadata.context_description: Democratic Party candidate Park Chan-dae dominates trader consensus for the June 3 Incheon mayoral election, reflecting his commanding polling leads over incumbent People Power Party Mayor Yoo Jeong-bok's third-term bid. A late-April Incheon Ilbo survey showed Park at 48.1%, solidifying his frontrunner status amid Democratic Party unity and regional momentum favoring the opposition. Park's April 22 candidacy launch emphasized ABC+E economic pledges, including biotech hubs and job creation, while his role as a Yoon impeachment key player appeals to core voters. Yoo's April 29 rally drew 7,000 supporters with global airport zone promises, yet polls indicate persistent gaps. Late-breaking scandals, campaign missteps, legal rulings, or incumbency rebound could still shift the first-past-the-post outcome.

markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in Hong Kong be 16°C on April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the lowest temperature recorded by the Hong Kong Observatory in degrees Celsius on 30 Apr '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the Hong Kong Observatory, specifically the "Absolute Daily Min (deg. C)" the specified date once information is finalized in the relevant "Daily Extract", available here: https://www.weather.gov.hk/en/cis/climat.htm

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures in Celsius to one decimal place (eg, 9.1°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will Trump post "Landslide" on Truth Social this week?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if @realDonaldTrump posts/truths the listed term between April 27, 2026, 12:00 AM ET and May 3, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, all text posted by the listed account in quote and reply posts/truths count toward a "Yes" resolution, but quoted posts/truths and reposts/reTruths will not count.

Text posted in images, memes, or other non-animated, non-video media that are not strictly text will qualify towards a "Yes" resolution only if the listed term is spelled out clearly and in full. (e.g., words spelled out in a letter posted as a .jpg will qualify, however a word posted as part of an animated .gif will not.)

Any plural or possessive forms of a listed term, as well as variance in capitalizations, will count toward the resolution of this market, regardless of context. Other forms of the listed term will NOT count.

Extraneous symbols being inserted into a word (ex: r@d1cal, for "radical") will disqualify it from counting toward a "Yes" resolution. Sigils at the beginnings of words, such as hashtags, "@" symbols, or denotations of currency, will not disqualify a term from counting towards a "Yes" resolution.

Misspellings or iterations of the listed term, including all grammatical or slang forms, or misspellings with extra, missing, or incorrect letters (ex: helloooooooo or heoll, for ‘hello’), will not count toward a “Yes” resolution, regardless of context or intent.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

The resolution source for this market will be Donald Trumps's verified Truth Social account: @realDonaldTrump

Please note, only the @realDonaldTrump verified Truth Social account counts for this market, regardless of the URL for this profile. If Donald Trump posts/truths from another account, it has no bearing on the resolution of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Donald Trump's frequent Truth Social posts amid escalating tensions over the Strait of Hormuz blockade by U.S. forces have dominated trader sentiment, with multiple entries April 27-30 featuring modified maps, ultimatums to Iran, and threats targeting power plants and bridges if demands go unmet. A recent security incident at the White House Correspondents' Dinner underscores heightened risks around public events. The week concludes May 3 alongside a golf tournament at Trump National Doral's Blue Monster course, potentially prompting resort boasts or victory claims reflective of ongoing foreign policy and domestic priorities.

events.title: Wuhan San Zhen FC vs. Qingdao Hainiu FC
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Chinese Super League game, scheduled for Wednesday, May 6, 2026 between Wuhan San Zhen FC and Qingdao Hainiu FC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Wuhan Three Towns holds a slim trader consensus edge at 42% implied probability as home favorites against Qingdao Hainiu in this Chinese Super League relegation six-pointer, buoyed by a dominant head-to-head record of four wins in six meetings, including perfect home form versus the visitors. Recent developments underscore the tight contest: Qingdao Hainiu's morale-boosting 4-1 upset over strong Shandong Taishan on April 26 has fueled their 31.5% chances despite a 15th-place table position, while Wuhan, 14th after a 4-0 drubbing by Shanghai Port last week, aims to leverage Wuhan Sports Center Stadium support. Draw pricing at 30% reflects both sides' inconsistent recent form amid low-scoring struggles.

markets.question: Will Starmer say "Shadow Secretary" during the next Prime Minister's Questions event?
markets.description:
Keir Starmer is scheduled to participate in the Prime Minister's Questions (PMQs) in the Commons Chamber in the Palace of Westminster on every Wednesday. (https://www.parliament.uk/visiting/visiting-and-tours/watch-committees-and-debates/prime-ministers-questions/).

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Keir Starmer says the listed term during the next Prime Minister's Question Time event he participates in. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Any usage of the term regardless of context will count toward the resolution of this market.

Pluralization/possessive of the term will count toward the resolution of this market, however other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

If no such Prime Minister's Question Time event happens by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No". If Starmer ceases to be Prime Minister for any length of time by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

This market is explicitly about scheduled events featuring Keir Starmer titled "Prime Minister's Question Time" (https://www.parallelparliament.co.uk/mp/keir-starmer/dept/cabinet-office/calendar). Speeches, events, or comments that occur outside of named, scheduled events will not qualify toward this market's resolution. If the event contains a Q&A, it will count toward the resolution of this market.

The resolution source will be video of the events. Only remarks which are broadcast or streamed live will count toward this market's resolution.

markets.question: Club Atlético de Madrid vs. RC Celta de Vigo: O/U 1.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming La Liga game between Club Atlético de Madrid and RC Celta de Vigo, scheduled for May 9 at 12:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Club Atlético de Madrid and RC Celta de Vigo combine to score 2 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 2, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on laliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Club Atlético de Madrid vs. RC Celta de Vigo - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the La Liga game, scheduled for May 9 at 12:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Atlético Madrid, entrenched in fourth place in the La Liga table after 33 matches, host sixth-placed RC Celta de Vigo on May 9 at Riyadh Air Metropolitano in a pivotal late-season clash for Champions League qualification. Los Colchoneros' recent form includes gritty home wins over Athletic Bilbao (3-2) and Getafe (1-0), bolstered by strong head-to-head dominance (19 wins in 30 meetings), though injuries to José Giménez (muscle), Dávid Hancko (ankle), and Pablo Barrios (thigh) temper squad depth. Celta, winless in their last five with a 1-0 loss to Barcelona, face away form woes despite a 1-1 draw at home earlier this season; key absences like Manu Fernández (muscle) add pressure amid their Europa League push. Home advantage and table stakes shape trader consensus.

markets.question: Fed rate hike in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the upper bound of the target federal funds rate is increased at any point between January 1, 2026 and the Fed's December 2026 meeting, currently scheduled for December 8-9, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the Fed has released its rate change decision following its December meeting.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Federal Reserve (https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: The 81.5% market-implied probability on Polymarket of no Federal Reserve rate hike in 2026 captures trader consensus after the April 29 FOMC meeting, where the federal funds target range held steady at 3.50%-3.75% for the third consecutive session amid elevated inflation and a resilient labor market. March 2026 CPI accelerated to 3.3% year-over-year from 2.4% prior, yet Chair Powell noted low job gains and no immediate calls for hikes, with futures pricing a flat path near 3.6% through year-end. March dot plot projections envision stability into 2027, outweighing hawkish risks from energy prices; traders eye April CPI release on May 12 and the June 17-18 FOMC for potential shifts.

markets.question: Jimmy Lai released by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Jimmy Lai is released from custody by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If Lai is released but remains under house arrest, the market will still resolve to "Yes".

If Lai is released on parole, bond, or any other condition that results in them leaving state custody, the market will resolve to "Yes".

Transporting Lai to another location of custody (e.g., a different prison, court, or hospital within the correctional system) will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Extradition of Lai to another country where he remains in custody will also not count.

Temporary outings from prison for purposes such as testifying in court, while still under the custody of correctional authorities, will not count as a release.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from relevant government authorities or corrections departments; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Hong Kong courts convicted pro-democracy publisher Jimmy Lai in December 2025 of conspiring to collude with foreign forces and publish seditious material under the national security law, imposing a 20-year prison sentence on February 9, 2026—the harshest yet for such offenses—which his legal team confirmed in March he will not appeal. A separate fraud conviction was overturned that month, but it leaves the national security term intact amid concurrent sentencing adjustments. Authorities' April 21 bid to seize over $16 million in Lai's assets underscores ongoing enforcement, with the 78-year-old remaining in solitary confinement despite health concerns and international calls for humanitarian parole. Traders' 97.5% consensus on "No" reflects the finalized ruling and lack of release pathways, though a sudden medical crisis, executive pardon, or diplomatic intervention could theoretically shift outcomes before June 30.

markets.question: Will Anthony Hudson win the 2026 Michigan Governor Republican primary election?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for Governor of Michigan, scheduled to take place on August 4, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Michigan Gubernatorial Republican Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Michigan Republican Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors self-funding businessman Perry Johnson at 60.5% implied probability to win Michigan's Republican gubernatorial primary on August 4, diverging from recent polls showing U.S. Rep. John James ahead—37% to Johnson's 20% in the April Detroit Regional Chamber survey and a near-tie at 21%-20% in Emerson polling. Johnson's aggressive ad spending, guaranteed ballot access after April petition filings, and pursuit of a Trump endorsement have driven market momentum amid a fragmented field of over 10 candidates splitting votes, including lower-tier contenders like Joyce Gipson and Evan Space. James benefits from incumbency and name recognition from prior Senate runs, but traders prioritize Johnson's financial edge; former Rep. Tom Leonard's April 23 dropout may consolidate support without shifting the lead.

markets.question: Will Analilia Mejia win the NJ-11 Special Election by 40% or more?
markets.description:
The special election for the seat for New Jersey's 11th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives is currently scheduled to take place on April 16, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the specified election.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first-place and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket.

If two or more candidates tie for the most valid votes in this election, and the tie is between two candidates listed, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the candidate whose listed last name comes first in alphabetical order. If the tie is between a listed candidate and an unlisted candidate, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed candidate. If the tie is between two or more unlisted candidates, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.nj.gov/state/.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

eventMetadata.context_description: Democrat Analilia Mejia's commanding lead in New Jersey's 11th Congressional District special election, held April 16 to replace Gov. Mikie Sherrill, has locked in trader consensus at 95.2% for a 20-25% margin of victory over Republican Joe Hathaway. With over 95% of votes counted, Mejia holds a roughly 20-point edge—about 10 points above Kamala Harris's 2024 presidential margin here—fueled by robust Democratic early voting and mail turnout exceeding expectations in this D+8 district. Late provisional ballots through late April further boosted Mejia in key areas like Passaic County. While certification awaits, shifts in the slim remaining uncounted votes or an improbable recount could narrow the margin below 20%, though the gap makes this unlikely.

eventMetadata.context_description: Challenger Oliver Adams Larkin's slight edge in trader consensus over incumbent Jared Moskowitz reflects progressive momentum from recent Democratic Socialists of America and Progressive Change Campaign Committee endorsements in late April, alongside media appearances criticizing Moskowitz's lack of in-person town halls and staunch pro-Israel positions amid district Democrats' calls for conditioning U.S. aid. A March internal poll showed Moskowitz leading 45%-11%, but grassroots fundraising—Larkin raised over $327,000 by March—and attacks on the incumbent's constituent services have narrowed the gap in this closed Democratic primary. The race remains tight due to splits between progressive voters prioritizing Medicare for All and a Green New Deal versus moderates valuing Moskowitz's experience in the competitive South Florida district. Upcoming candidate forums, new polls, and Q2 fundraising reports ahead of the August 18 primary could tip the balance.

markets.question: Metamask FDV above $500M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Metamask's token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Metamask doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Houston Dynamo vs. Colorado Rapids SC: O/U 4.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming MLS game between Houston Dynamo and Colorado Rapids SC, scheduled for May 2 at 8:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Houston Dynamo and Colorado Rapids SC combine to score 5 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 5, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on mlssoccer.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Houston Dynamo vs. Colorado Rapids SC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the MLS game, scheduled for May 2 at 8:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Colorado Rapids host Houston Dynamo in an MLS Western Conference clash at Dick's Sporting Goods Park, with Rapids sitting 7th in early standings on 9 points versus Dynamo's 9th-place 6 points after mixed starts—Rapids with wins over Portland but losses away, Dynamo showing home strength but stumbling 4-3 at Dallas last weekend. Houston's defense is depleted: center back Lucas Halter out injured, Erik Sviatchenko suspended after red card. Rapids counter absences like Reggie Cannon (ankle), Connor Ronan (leg), testing backlines. Even head-to-head history and Colorado's altitude/home form shape trader consensus on goals and clean sheets amid uncertain starting XIs.

markets.question: Will Silver (SI) settle over $75 on the final trading day of June 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official CME settlement price for the Active Month of Silver futures on the final trading day of June 2026 is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Silver (SI) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (March, May, July, September, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month becomes a non-active month effective on its First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days during June on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Silver (SI) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Silver spot prices have rallied to around $76 per ounce, propelled by persistent supply deficits—now in their sixth consecutive year per the World Silver Survey 2026—and surging industrial demand from solar photovoltaics and electronics, which accounts for over half of total consumption. June 2026 silver futures (SI) trade near $76, signaling trader consensus for sustained levels amid USD depreciation and softer oil prices in the past 48 hours that enhanced precious metals appeal. Fed funds rate cut expectations further bolster sentiment, though volatility looms from inflation trajectory. Key catalysts ahead include May 10 CPI data, June nonfarm payrolls, and the June 11-12 FOMC meeting, where policy signals could sway dollar strength and risk appetite impacting silver's end-June settlement.

markets.question: Will Xi Jinping win the Nobel Peace Prize in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize, as announced by the Norwegian Nobel Committee.

If multiple listed individuals or entities jointly receive the prize, the market will resolve to a single winner. If Donald Trump, Volodymyr Zelenskyy,  Benjamin Netanyahu, Vladimir Putin, or Elon Musk are among the recipients, the market will resolve to the highest-ranked among them in that exact order of precedence. If none of those five are among the winners and the prize is awarded jointly to at least one listed individual and at least one listed organization, the market will resolve in favor of the individual. If no prioritized individuals are among the winners and all listed recipients are of the same type (all individuals or all organizations), the market will resolve to the person whose last name, or the entity’s name, comes first in alphabetical order as listed in this market.

If no official announcement regarding the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize has been made by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other."

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement from the Norwegian Nobel Committee.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects high uncertainty in the early 2026 Nobel Peace Prize race, with Yulia Navalnaya's 7.5% implied probability edging Volodymyr Zelenskyy (6.8%) and Donald Trump (6.5%) amid 287 total nominees announced April 30 by the Norwegian Nobel Committee. Recent buzz stems from confirmed high-profile nominations—Trump backed by Pakistan, Cambodia, Israel leaders, and U.S. Rep. Anna Paulina Luna's fresh submission—juxtaposed against Navalnaya's rising symbol of Russian opposition and Zelenskyy's Ukraine resilience narrative. UNRWA and Qatar's Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani gain traction from Gaza mediation efforts, while Pope Leo XIV draws Vatican diplomacy speculation. No dominant frontrunner emerges without precursor signals; watch for shortlist hints and geopolitical shifts before the October 10 announcement.

markets.question: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices "No" at 72.5% implied probability for a U.S. AI safety bill—requiring signed federal law with provisions like prohibitions on AI model creation, training limits, usage restrictions, or mandatory human-in-the-loop oversight—before 2027, reflecting persistent congressional gridlock in the divided 119th Congress. The Trump administration's March 20 National AI Legislative Framework urged light-touch federal rules prioritizing innovation and state preemption, stalling comprehensive safety measures amid competing priorities like appropriations. Dozens of bills, including the AI Guardrails Act (S.4113), Future of AI Innovation Act (S.3952), and late-April's omnibus American Leadership in AI Act, remain mired in committees without floor votes. States like California (SB 1047) and New York (RAISE Act) have enacted their own rules, underscoring federal inaction, with slim lame-duck prospects given historical tech regulation challenges.

markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in Shanghai be 13°C on May 3?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the lowest temperature recorded at the Shanghai Pudong International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 3 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the lowest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Shanghai Pudong International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/shanghai/ZSPD.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket implies a 37% probability for Shanghai's lowest temperature reaching 15°C on May 3, with 14–17°C outcomes tightly clustered at 26–28%, reflecting ensemble forecast spread from the China Meteorological Administration (CMA) and international models like ECMWF and GFS. This positioning stems from a lingering upper-level trough ushering northerly winds and cooler continental air masses into the Yangtze River Delta, suppressing nighttime lows below the early May historical average of 16°C. Key differentiators include uncertainty in low-level cloud cover—clear skies enabling radiative cooling toward 14°C, versus scattered clouds trapping heat up to 17°C—coupled with calm winds minimizing mixing. CMA's latest guidance centers on 15°C, but traders eye evening 00Z model runs for potential shifts ahead of resolution based on official Shanghai Pudong Airport observations.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects a tightly contested Eastern Conference Finals MVP market, with Donovan Mitchell (29%), Brandon Ingram (28.5%), Jayson Tatum (24%), and Jalen Brunson (22%) clustered due to unresolved first-round playoff series keeping multiple stars' paths open. Cleveland Cavaliers hold a 3-2 edge over Toronto Raptors after Mitchell's scoring outbursts, including 32 points in Game 1, though Ingram's recent Eastern Conference Player of the Week form persists at high odds despite his right heel inflammation sidelining him in Game 5 and clouding Game 6. Detroit Pistons' Cade Cunningham exploded for a franchise-record 45 points in Game 5 to force a Game 7 versus Orlando Magic, while Boston Celtics remain locked in a tied series and New York Knicks advanced past Atlanta behind Brunson, fueling uncertainty in ECF matchups and star impacts.

markets.question: Will Khvicha Kvaratskhelia be the 2025/2026 top UCL goal scorer?

If multiple players tie for the most goals in the 2025/2026 UEFA Champions League season, the market will resolve based on most assists, then most games played, and finally the player's last name alphabetically if ties persist.

If a player's team is eliminated, and they are not in the goal lead, the market for that player may resolve to "No".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the UEFA Champions League. However, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Harry Kane sits close at 13 goals in 12 appearances for Bayern Munich (40.5% implied), having narrowed the gap with key strikes in their semi-final first leg against PSG four days ago, showcasing his clinical finishing amid Bayern's favorable path. Trailing contenders like Khvicha Kvaratskhelia (10 goals, PSG) and Julian Alvarez face steeper climbs due to fewer goals and teams' advancement uncertainties in the knockout stages.

markets.question: Will Amazon have the #1 AI model at the end of May 2026 (Style Control On)?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank (Style Control On) based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

Without imminent releases from challengers like xAI or ByteDance, the lead appears resilient, though Google previews signal potential volatility before resolution.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Chicago be between 34-35°F on May 4?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Chicago O'Hare Intl Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 4 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Chicago O'Hare Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/il/chicago/KORD.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

This positioning reflects a persistent upper-level trough over the Midwest anchoring unseasonably cool conditions well below the 66°F climatological normal for early May, yet partly sunny skies, moderate winds, and ample solar insolation ensure highs comfortably exceed the threshold. Over the past 48 hours, model runs have shown tight agreement with minimal spread in stable synoptic patterns following recent frost threats. Realistic challenges—a sudden northerly cold surge or prolonged overcast reducing peaks by 5-7°F—lack current support from observations; watch NOAA's afternoon forecast discussions for updates.

markets.question: Will Robinhood Markets, Inc. (HOOD) hit (HIGH) $90 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Robinhood Markets, Inc. (HOOD) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Robinhood Markets, Inc. (HOOD) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.HOOD%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.HOOD%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

markets.question: Games Total: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the Valorant match between Paper Rex and DRX in the VCT Pacific Group Alpha, initially scheduled for May 3 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Over" if Paper Rex and DRX play 3 or more maps in this series.

If fewer than 3 maps are played, this market will resolve to "Under".

Maps won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the total, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching map being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://vlr.gg. However, if https://vlr.gg has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: Valorant: Paper Rex vs DRX (BO3) - VCT Pacific Group Alpha
events.description:
This market refers to the Valorant match between Paper Rex and DRX in the VCT Pacific Group Alpha, initially scheduled for May 3 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Paper Rex" if Paper Rex win the match against DRX.

This market will resolve to "DRX" if DRX win the match against Paper Rex.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://vlr.gg. However, if https://vlr.gg has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: In VCT Pacific Stage 1 Group Alpha's decisive BO3 for the top seed and playoffs bye, undefeated Kiwoom DRX (4-0, +17 RD) faces Paper Rex (3-1, +32 RD) on May 3. DRX's momentum stems from a recent reverse sweep over Gen.G, maintaining their flawless win streak amid roster tweaks like Hermes' return from reserve. Paper Rex boasts dominant recent form, including 2-0 sweeps of Global Esports and Team Secret after an early loss, plus superior round differential and a 2-1 lower bracket elimination of DRX at Pacific Kickoff. The storied RX derby features stylistic clashes, with f0rsakeN's flex versatility and Hyunmin's dueling key; trader consensus reflects tight head-to-head history and seeding stakes.

markets.question: Will SpaceX's market cap be between $1.8T and $2.0T at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on SpaceX's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no SpaceX IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO before 2028".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on SpaceX’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NV-01 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NV-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Will Jorge Rodríguez be the leader of Venezuela end of 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the individual who officially holds the position of the head of state of Venezuela on Dec 31, 2026 at 12 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, "officially holds" refers to the individual that was formally appointed, confirmed (if confirmation is required), and sworn in as the head of state of Venezuela or otherwise confirmed by official government information as being the head of state of Venezuela. 

If the Venezuelan government does not clearly state who is the head of state, the market will resolve to the individual who is listed as the Head of State by the UN (see: https://www.un.org/dgacm/en/content/protocol/hshgnfa).  

In the event that more than one official head of state is listed by the Venezuelan  government, this market will resolve to the individual who is listed as having primary status. If no distinction is made, the market will resolve to the individual who first assumed the position. 

If no individual holds the position this market will resolve to “No Head of State”. 

The following do NOT constitute "officially holding" the role: nominated, announced, or designated as the head of state of Venezuela but appointment not yet effective; appointed with an effective date after Dec 31, 2026; previously served as the head of state of Venezuela but term has expired, resigned, or been terminated before Dec 31, 2026; serving as the head of state of Venezuela in a consultant, contractor, or unofficial capacity; named as successor or heir apparent to the head of state of Venezuela but not yet appointed; or holding a different role with oversight of the head of state of Venezuela but not the specified position itself.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the UN recognized government of Venezuela. If the Government of Venezuela does not clearly state who is the head of state, information from the UN and a consensus of credible reporting may be used. 

Note: this market is mutually exclusive.

Vice President Delcy Rodríguez, sworn in as interim president via Supreme Court ruling, commands 22% implied probability after extending her 90-day mandate past April without elections—bolstered by March military reshuffles firing Maduro allies like the defense minister while retaining key figures—and National Assembly President Jorge Rodríguez's February rejection of snap presidential votes. Opposition leader María Corina Machado trails at 9%, unifying anti-regime forces and planning a year-end return to push for polls, though facing bans, repression, and no clear electoral path amid uncertainty over U.S. influence and transition timelines.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket overwhelmingly favors a high temperature of 48°F or higher in Chicago on May 4 (99% implied probability), driven by the latest National Weather Service guidance and GFS/ECMWF ensemble consensus projecting peaks around 53-55°F at O'Hare International Airport. This positioning reflects a persistent upper-level trough over the Midwest anchoring unseasonably cool conditions well below the 66°F climatological normal for early May, yet partly sunny skies, moderate winds, and ample solar insolation ensure highs comfortably exceed the threshold. Realistic challenges—a sudden northerly cold surge or prolonged overcast reducing peaks by 5-7°F—lack current support from observations; watch NOAA's afternoon forecast discussions for updates.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the WI-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the WI-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

A crowded Democratic primary featuring eight low-funded candidates, led by Bradley Smith's modest $53,000 cash on hand, limits the party's 16.5% odds amid historical district losses. Independent Mike Thurow's union-backed challenge accounts for remaining probability, with Republican and Democratic primaries set for August 11 ahead of the November 3 general election.
